summary: Replaced the `python3`-only JSON fixture rewriting in `scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` with Node-based equivalents so the native runtime test no longer depends on `python3` being present. The change is small and local to the failing verification path identified by prior feedback.
tests_run: Targeted Node fixture check exercising the new JSON rewrite logic for upgrade fingerprints and `bodyText` stripping
Attempted `bash scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` (blocked in this environment at `rustc: command not found`)
residual_risks: I could not complete the end-to-end native runtime script in this environment because `rustc` is missing, so the full path remains unverified here.

feedback.summary: This closes the prior environment-specific verifier finding about an unconditional `python3` dependency in `scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` by using Node, which is already part of the repo’s test toolchain.
ergonomics: The fix is isolated to the test harness and does not widen compiler/runtime scope.
Using Node keeps the JSON fixture transforms aligned with other repo scripts that already parse JSON with Node.
follow_ups: Re-run `bash scripts/test-native-runtime.sh` in an environment with `rustc` available.
If verifier environments may also lack Node in the future, add an explicit upfront tool check with a clearer failure message.
warnings: The worktree already had unrelated modifications and generated-file changes; I did not revert them.
I did not run `bash scripts/verify-all.sh` per task instructions.
